column5: Test steps
column6: Expected Result

column5:
1. Inspect build.gradle or Manifest.
2. Verify targetSdkVersion.
column6: targetSdkVersion is set to 36.

column5:
1. Capture BLE traffic during data exchange.
2. Verify packet headers and structure.
column6: Communication adheres to Protocol V4.2.15 specifications.

column5:
1. Launch App and Scan for devices.
2. Observe if SV95 appears.
column6: SV95 is discovered. Verification ensures DFU check logic no longer blocks SV95.

column5:
1. Observe SV95 Home Card.
2. Compare values with hardware display.
column6: Card displays Overall Acceleration for X, Y and Z axes correctly.

column5:
1. Observe SV95 Home Card.
2. Observe the temperature field.
column6: Temperature reading is displayed next to vibration data.

column5:
1. Trigger a high vibration/temp reading.
2. Observe value color.
column6: Values change color based on thresholds (e.g. Yellow for Alert  Red for Danger).

column5:
1. Verify the absence of the 'Report' button.
2. Observe the new Trend Card.
column6: Report button is removed; Trend Card displays historical data plots.

column5:
1. Apply time/date filters.
2. Apply value filters.
column6: Trend chart updates to show only filtered data segments.

column5: 1. View the live/trend chart.
column6: Chart displays two Y-axes to accommodate vibration and temperature units simultaneously.

column5:
1. Tap and hold on a chart data point.
2. Drag across the timeline.
column6: A marker appears showing the exact value and timestamp for the selected point.

column5:
1. Open Alarm Settings.
2. Select Large, Medium, or Small machine group.
column6: Thresholds are updated according to the selected ISO 10816 category.

column5:
1. Manually enter Alert and Danger limits.
2. Save settings.
column6: Limits are saved and applied to Home Card colors and Notifications.

column5:
1. Navigate to 'FILES' tab.
2. Verify 'Gallery' button is hidden.
3. Tap 'Recording List'.
column6: Gallery button is hidden for SV95; Recording List is accessible.

column5:
1. Select log from list.
2. Tap 'Download'.
column6: File is downloaded and accessible for viewing within the App.

column5:
1. Switch OA unit.
2. Check Home Card and Chart.
column6: Values and labels convert correctly; Alert thresholds are scaled.

column5:
1. Switch Temp unit.
2. Check Home Card and Chart.
column6: Temperature values and labels convert correctly.

column2: Temp Unit Switch - Alarm Clear Logic
column5:
1. Switch Temperature unit (°C to °F or vice-versa).
2. Check Alarm settings.
column6: All existing Temperature Alarm settings are cleared (App standard logic).

column5: 1. Verify entry points for Wi-Fi, MQTT, and Schedule.
column6: Sub-pages are accessible and display current device settings.

column5:
1. Connect SV95.
2. Verify device clock sync.
column6: App sends 'Set RTC' command immediately upon connection.

column5:
1. Connect SV95.
2. Monitor BLE commands.
column6: App automatically sends 'Get Wi-Fi'  'Get MQTT'  and 'Get Schedule' info.

column5:
1. Connect new SV95.
2. App detects null/empty schedule.
column6: App calls 'Set Schedule' with default: 2s Sampling  2m Interval  3 years duration.

column5:
1. Tap 'Apply Wi-Fi settings from existing device'.
2. Select source device.
column6: Wi-Fi SSID and Password are copied to the target SV95.

column5:
1. Tap 'Apply MQTT settings from existing device'.
2. Select source device.
column6: MQTT Broker Port  and Topic settings are copied to the target SV95.

column5:
1. Switch OS to Dark Mode.
2. Navigate through App.
column6: All SV95-specific screens adhere to Dark Mode theme (contrast/readability).

column5:
1. Launch App on Tablet.
2. Verify layout and Dark Mode.
column6: App is functional on Tablet; UI elements are properly scaled.

column5:
1. Navigate to More page.
2. Observe UI/BLE refresh.
column6: App re-sends 'Get Info' commands (Wi-Fi/MQTT/Sched) every time More page is entered.

column5:
1. Trigger Alarm (Alert or Danger).
2. Observe system notification.
column6: Notification format: "%Device% measurement %val% is higher than [alert|danger] value %limit%".

column5:
1. Verify dependency versions.
2. Check for Sign Key integrity.
column6: App passes security compliance and uses the correct signing key.

column5:
1. Change Sampling to 5s.
2. Reboot SV95 and reconnect.
column6: Modified schedule settings persist on the device hardware.

column5: 1. Drag chart marker rapidly.
column6: Marker remains responsive and updates values without lag.

column5: 1. Tap 'Full-screen' icon on chart.
column6: Chart expands to fill screen; landscape orientation is supported.

column5:
1. Change OA unit (mm/s to inch/s).
2. Check ISO thresholds.
column6: ISO thresholds (e.g. 2.8 mm/s) are correctly converted to inch/s.

column5:
1. View MQTT page.
2. Observe status.
column6: App displays whether the SV95 is successfully connected to the MQTT broker.

column5:
1. Simulate BLE error during RTC sync.
2. Observe App behavior.
column6: App attempts to retry the connection sequence or shows a clear error state.

column5: 1. Measure cold start time.
column6: App launches within performance guidelines on API Level 36.
